memberdef.type: std::conditional_t< std::is_lvalue_reference_v< Ref >, Ref, std::remove_cvref_t< Ref > && >
memberdef.name: ParallelCollectArgT

memberdef.type: ResultValueT< std::invoke_result_t< std::remove_reference_t< Task > &, ParallelCollectArgT< std::ranges::range_reference_t< InputRange > > > >
memberdef.name: ParallelCollectValueT

briefdescription.para: Check if an equality delete file can contain deletes for a data file.

memberdef.argsstring: (const Literal &data_lower, const Literal &data_upper, const Literal &delete_lower, const Literal &delete_upper)
memberdef.name: RangesOverlap

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::map< int32_t, int64_t > &null_counts, const std::map< int32_t, int64_t > &value_counts, int32_t field_id, bool is_required)
memberdef.name: AllNull

briefdescription.para: Check if a value count map indicates all values are null.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::map< int32_t, int64_t > &null_counts, int32_t field_id, bool is_required)
memberdef.name: AllNonNull

briefdescription.para: Check if all values are non-null.

memberdef.argsstring: (const std::map< int32_t, int64_t > &null_counts, int32_t field_id, bool is_required)
memberdef.name: ContainsNull

briefdescription.para: Check if the column contains any null values.
Hot-path accessor for the default logger.

detaileddescription.para: Returns a reference to a thread-local cached shared_ptr that is refreshed only when the default logger has changed (no lock / no refcount churn in steady state). The reference is valid for the duration of the calling statement.

briefdescription.para: Build a LogMessage from the already-formatted text and dispatch it.

detaileddescription.para: Declared ICEBERG_EXPORT because the logging macros expand into this call in consumer translation units.

briefdescription.para: Emit a fixed fallback record when formatting threw.

detaileddescription.para: noexcept, allocation-light (small/SSO literal), performs no std::format, and does not recurse so the macro's "logging never throws" guarantee holds even when a format argument throws.

std::format requires a compile-time format string; this routes a runtime string through std::vformat. Args are bound as named lvalues and the arg-store is held in a named variable so it outlives the vformat call (C++23 make_format_args rejects rvalues P2905 / LWG3631).

memberdef.argsstring: (Logger &logger, LogLevel level, const std::source_location &loc, std::format_string< Args... > fmt, Args &&... args) noexcept
memberdef.name: FormatAndEmit

briefdescription.para: Shared gate -> format -> emit body for the function-style Log() API.

detaileddescription.para: Formats only when the logger is enabled for level, and never throws (a formatting failure routes to EmitFormatError, matching the macros).
